executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture-owned gates remain green. Whole-stack certification still blocked by external marketing outcome evidence. Health monitor shows blocked-channel-recovery timeout escalated + marketing_independent_verification still stale.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Resolve blocked-channel-recovery timeout escalation and get fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus live artifact cross-checks
live_job_names: Push research findings to git repo

findings.title: Marketing remains externally red on outcome evidence
findings.mechanism: Marketing independent verification still fails closed because primary-repo adoption is measurement-pending.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence for Codeberg-primary adoption is still missing.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the marketing owner loop produce fresh measurable outcome evidence, then rerun marketing independent verification before calling the whole stack green.

findings.title: Health monitor shows escalated blocked-channel-recovery timeout
findings.mechanism: Health monitor now reports 4 issues including blocked-channel-recovery timeout with escalation_required flag (appears twice — possible duplicate artifact).
findings.root_cause: blocked-channel-recovery cron job previously timed out; escalation flag now tripped.
findings.recommended_fix: Verify blocked-channel-recovery has cleared (live cron shows 0 errors now). If resolved, the health monitor artifact needs a refresh to clear the stale escalation issue.

findings.title: Live Gateway topology is clean — 24 enabled, 0 disabled, 0 errors, 0 running
findings.mechanism: Direct live cron inspection shows full coherence with no runtime errors or disabled jobs.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep direct cron inspection as the source of truth on each watchdog run.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path is green on freshness and ownership gates
findings.mechanism: Loop integrity shows both ralph-docs-watchdog and agent-architecture-watchdog as 'ok'. Architecture verifier passes closed on stale signoff. Independent verification confirms qualified_pass.
findings.root_cause: Architecture-side gates are healthy; remaining risk is external.
findings.recommended_fix: Rerun independent verification after each material architecture artifact refresh.

findings.title: Persisted disabled jobs remain history only, not live runtime blockers
findings.mechanism: Disabled entries still exist in jobs.json history, but live Gateway topology currently exposes zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Historical scheduler records persist after live job removal/disable cleanup.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live runtime topology in every audit.

findings.title: Loop 'pypi-auto-unblocker' has NO self-improvement mandate
findings.mechanism: Script has no self-improvement mandate. When outcomes are flat, this loop will repeat the same tactics forever without improving or redesigning its approach.
findings.root_cause: Loop was created without a self-improvement mandate or a third-party verification requirement.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a self_improvement_mandate section to the loop script that: 1) Detects when outcomes are flat for N consecutive runs, 2) Triggers a redesign pass: new agents, prompt rewrites, cron changes, or path retirement, 3) Registers the loop in the self_improvement_loops.json registry with checker/runner/verifier, 4) Requires independent third-party signoff before marking the loop healthy again.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Resolve blocked-channel-recovery timeout escalation — live cron shows 0 errors now, but health monitor still carries the stale escalation issue
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Escalation flag in health monitor may cascade into spurious downstream blocker signals.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Clears the artificial escalation noise from the health monitor so remaining blockers stay localized to real external issues.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Get a fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Marketing still blocks whole-stack certification even though architecture-owned gates are coherent.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining adoption blocker or preserves truthful fail-closed outcome ownership.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture live-topology verification tied to openclaw cron list --json on every watchdog run
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current live inspection is clean and should remain the source of truth instead of persisted-history drift.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Stops stale topology claims from reappearing in architecture reports.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Refreshed the audit against the current live view: 24 enabled jobs, 0 disabled, 0 running, 0 live last-error jobs. Previous run snapshot had 3 running + 1 error (blocked-channel-recovery timeout); those have cleared.
Health monitor now shows 4 issues (was 3), with blocked-channel-recovery_escalation appearing twice. Live cron shows 0 errors — likely stale health-monitor artifact that needs refresh.
Reconfirmed both ralph-docs-watchdog and agent-architecture-watchdog are 'ok' with verifierContractExternalized=true.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification found architecture blockers that prevent a healthy verifier pass.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh architecture independent verification against the refreshed live topology.
Fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement.
Health monitor refresh to clear stale blocked-channel-recovery escalation duplicates.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing remains red on Codeberg-primary outcome evidence
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Architecture-owned runtime checks are coherent, but marketing independent verification still fails closed because primary-repo movement is still measurement-pending.

notes: Architecture green here means the architecture-owned verifier path is coherent; it does not mean the whole stack is green.
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are 0.
Independent live inspection in this snapshot saw 24 total jobs, 24 enabled, 0 disabled, 0 running, 0 live last-error jobs — cleaner than the previous snapshot.
The remaining blocker is external marketing outcome evidence, not architecture runtime drift.
Health monitor has 4 issues with blocked-channel-recovery_escalation appearing twice — likely a stale duplicate that needs a health-monitor refresh to clear.
No live timeout-budget repair was applied in this watchdog run.
